component.name: Phone Mirror
Telefonspiegelung
Espejo del teléfono
Miroir du téléphone
फ़ोन मिरर
Klonowanie Telefonu
Espelhamento do telefone
Зеркало телефона
手机镜像
component.summary: Wire-free Android screen mirroring and control
Espejo y control inalámbrico de la pantalla de Android
Recopie et contrôle d'écran Android sans fil
वायर-मुक्त Android स्क्रीन मिररिंग और नियंत्रण
Bezprzewodowe klonowanie i sterowanie ekranem Androida
Espelhamento e controle de tela Android sem fios
Беспроводное зеркалирование и управление экраном Android
无线 Android 屏幕镜像与控制

description.p: Phone Mirror displays your Android phone's screen on the Linux desktop and lets you control it with your mouse and keyboard, wire-free over your local network. It is built natively for the modern desktop with GTK4 and libadwaita, runs on Wayland, and decodes the video stream with FFmpeg.

ul.li: Live, low-latency mirroring of the phone screen onto the desktop
Mouse, scroll and keyboard control, including UTF-8 text input
The phone's audio played through the desktop's speakers
Automatic device discovery on the local network (mDNS/DNS-SD)
One-time pairing with persisted, reconnectable devices
HiDPI-correct input mapping and a distraction-free, undecorated mirror window

description.p: First stable release, focused on making shutdown, reconnect, and disconnect paths safer for regular use.

ul.li: Prevented queued decoder frames from targeting a session that is already being destroyed.
Avoided sending input-release messages through a freed control socket after spontaneous disconnects.
Cleaned up input controllers when leaving the live mirroring state, so the next session starts with fresh control state.
Detached the video view during window disposal to avoid stale session-to-widget references.
Made scrcpy-server argument construction tolerant of omitted optional bitrate or virtual-display values.
Updated version, packaging, translation metadata, README localization details, and architecture notes for the stable release.
Waits for a freshly connected wireless adb device to become ready before pushing the mirror server, reducing races on cold adb starts.
Removes stale adb port forwards before creating a new mirror forward, so failed previous attempts are less likely to block the next connection.
Shows clearer "No phone found" guidance with a shortcut back to the setup guide when automatic reconnect cannot find the phone.
Improves resize handling after in-window dialogs while mirroring.
Adds a floating Unlock button over the live mirror whenever the connected phone is detected on its lockscreen.
Polls keyguard state during mirroring to drive the Unlock button and to avoid blanking the phone screen before it is usable.
Adds a setup-guide step for disabling Android's adb authorization timeout, helping keep paired computers trusted for longer.
Refreshes icons, preview imagery, and translated strings.
Prompts for a one-time PIN when a connected phone is locked but no saved PIN is available.
Lets users unlock a live mirrored phone without storing the PIN on the computer.
Runs manual unlock attempts asynchronously and clears entered PIN text after use.
Auto-unlock now stops after a few unsuccessful PIN attempts, so an incorrect saved PIN cannot keep trying until the phone locks you out.
If the saved PIN does not work, the app now explains what happened and offers a quick way to enter the correct PIN.
Reconnecting to a paired phone should be more reliable because the app ignores old network records that no longer reach the phone.
Improves wireless device discovery by validating saved/manual endpoints and skipping stale mDNS records.
Scans the paired phone's host for Android's dynamic wireless debugging port when only the host address is known.
Accepts host:port input directly in the connection dialog, including bracketed IPv6 addresses.
Moves auto-unlock to run after the stream starts and shows an "Unlocking" overlay instead of leaving users on a blank screen.
Improves mirror-window sizing limits and aspect-locked resizing.

description.p: Expanded localization metadata and catalogs with German, Spanish, French, Hindi, Brazilian Portuguese, Russian, and Simplified Chinese, alongside the existing Polish translation.

ul.li: Added gettext localization support with a Polish translation.
Introduced the guided first-run setup wizard for preparing Android wireless debugging and pairing in-app.
Updated the README, desktop metadata, and packaging for the new setup and localization flow.

description.p: Initial development release: working UI, device discovery and pairing, and the live mirroring pipeline.
